                                     ABSTRACT31

                   Computer Aided Design (CAD) engineers typically do not achieve their best prototypes in a single
                        attempt. Instead, they iterate and refine their designs to achieve an optimal solution through multiple
                         revisions. This traditional approach, though effective, is time-consuming and relies heavily on the
                        expertise of skilled engineers. To address these challenges, we introduce Query2CAD, a novel
                    framework to generate CAD designs. The framework uses a large language model to generate[cs.LG]                executable CAD macros. Additionally, Query2CAD refines the generation of the CAD model with
                       the help of its self-refinement loops. Query2CAD operates without supervised data or additional
                           training, using the LLM as both a generator and a refiner. The refiner leverages feedback generated by
                        the BLIP2 model, and to address false negatives, we have incorporated human-in-the-loop feedback
                         into our system. Additionally, we have developed a dataset that encompasses most operations used
                         in CAD model designing and have evaluated our framework using this dataset. Our findings reveal
                          that when we used GPT-4 Turbo as our language model, the architecture achieved a success rate of
                  53.6% on the first attempt. With subsequent refinements, the success rate increased by 23.1%. In
                          particular, the most significant improvement in the success rate was observed with the first iteration
                       of the refinement. With subsequent refinements, the accuracy of the correct designs did not improve
                           significantly. We have open-sourced our data, model, and code.1


          1  Introduction

         CAD has revolutionized the way engineers, architects, and designers conceptualize and design prototypes and products.arXiv:2406.00144v1     CAD designing is a very important step to design prototypes and also it helps in understanding the potential failures that
             might occur during the manufacturing phase. Over the years, CAD softwares have evolved significantly and become
              very powerful. CAD designing was always done by a CAD engineer as it required a deep understanding of complex
               operations [1]. The reasoning capabilities of LLMs have simplified many complex problems [2]. However, using LLMs
                to aid in the generation of CAD models remains largely untapped. The integration of natural language processing into
         CAD can significantly improve the design time. It can also make complex CAD software more accessible to people
              with less experience in CAD designing.

            Over the past decade, deep learning methods had significant success in understanding data formats like mesh
                 [3], voxels [4], and point clouds.  With the advent of diffusion models, a significant shift occurred towards
              image-conditioned 3D generative modeling, particularly enhancing the capabilities in point clouds and neural fields
                 [5]. A critical limitation identified in these studies was the necessity for precise camera poses, which constrained the
                applicability and flexibility of the models. Recent innovations eliminated the need for camera poses [6], but the major
             drawback of such data format is the problems that it might pose during feature extraction. These extracted features are

                 1github.com/akshay140601/Query2CADFigure 1: Proposed architecture of Query2CAD. The user query is passed to an LLM that generates a Python macro to
generate the corresponding CAD model. The isometric view is captured, and refinement is performed if the VQA score
does not cross the threshold. The loop is run for a maximum of 3 times.


critical to the CAD model, and converting them to the right data format, such as triangular meshes, can be expensive
and can also lead to information loss [7].

In this work, we introduce Query2CAD, a novel architecture that returns a CAD model similar to the user’s
prompt. We plan to exploit the capabilities of language models to generate Python programs (Macro) that can be
executed in the FreeCAD software to obtain 3D CAD models. A designer refines his design multiple times based
on the feedback given to him until he designs a CAD model that closely aligns with what the user has asked for. To
mimic this behavior, our architecture has a refinement loop that refines its generation with respect to the user prompt.
We incorporated a caption-generating model to provide feedback on what was designed, and, to encounter the false
negatives of this model, we have also incorporated human-in-the-loop feedback. We have used Visual Question
Answering Score [8] to check if we have reached an optimal CAD design. At a high level, our architecture takes input
from the user (for instance, "Give me the CAD design of a water bottle") and passes it to a language model, which
generates a working CAD design. Throughout this process, users do not require any prior knowledge of CAD software
and can interact with the system using natural language. A brief overview of our architecture is shown in Figure 1.


2  Related works

Generating 3D models: Early explorations of generating 3D models used variational autoencoders [9] [10]. However,
this just worked for simple models, and the 3D models generated were very similar to that of the training corpus and
failed at generating models outside of the training corpus [11]. Later researchers have investigated 3D GANs [12] [13],
but the main drawback of generating 3D models using GAN is its instability during training [14]. However, recently
image conditioned 3D modeling has shown good results in generating 3D models[15] but the precision is not very good
when compared to that of a CAD model. In our research, we aim to advance beyond point cloud generation to focus on
3D CAD model creation. CAD models are pivotal to the manufacturing process and facilitate the direct transfer of
data to production machinery. While 3D models generated by various image-to-3D diffusion models have accurately
captured the geometry of points in the 3D CAD models [16], CAD models provide more profound utility by enabling
an advanced level of design, analysis, and manufacturability.

Innovative approaches taken by people that can assist in 3D CAD model generation:   Recent studies,
such as those by [17], have highlighted the capability of language models in generating conceptual designs. These
studies demonstrate that language models can effectively reason and produce detailed concept designs that can
significantly assist design engineers in their work. Some works [18] integrated imagery of the model into their 3D CAD
design software, enhancing the design process by inspiring more innovative ideas among designers as they create their
models. However, our research aims to directly generate 3D CAD models based on prompts provided by users.

Self-refinement Loops: The iterative process used in human problem-solving, such as in CAD design, typi-
cally involves creating an initial model and refining  it based on identified flaws, a method rooted in broader


                                               2problem-solving strategies discussed by [19]. Unlike this human approach, most current language models generate
outputs in a single step, which contrasts with the iterative nature of human task performance [20], [21]. Although
some research, such as [22], has explored post-inference editing, these methods typically do not support multi-step
refinement. Recent advancements include the development of models that integrate iterative feedback mechanisms to
enhance output quality [23]. This model iteratively refines its output by reprocessing its initial output through the
model to obtain and incorporate feedback internally. We aim to incorporate this approach into our architecture, thereby
emulating the way CAD designers iteratively ref

function.arguments:
{"artifact_type": "table", "description": "Create a comprehensive reference table of ~15 papers on generative CAD / AI-assisted CAD, organized with columns: Reference # | Citation | Category | Summary (3-5 sentences) | Relevance to Python/Code-Based Generative CAD Pipeline for Powder-Dosing Device.

Include the following papers in this order:

1. Wu et al. (2021) - DeepCAD: ICCV 2021. First deep generative model for CAD sequences using Transformer. Dataset of 178,238 models. Category: Landmark Dataset / Foundational Model.

2. Willis et al. (2021) - Fusion 360 Gallery: ACM TOG 2021. 8,625 human design sequences in sketch+extrude language with Fusion 360 Gym environment. Category: Landmark Dataset / Benchmark.

3. Seff et al. (2021) - Vitruvion: arXiv 2021. Autoregressive generative model for parametric CAD sketches with constraints, trained on SketchGraphs (15M sketches from Onshape). Category: Constraint-Aware Sketch Generation / Dataset.

4. Para et al. (2021) - SketchGen: NeurIPS 2021. Generates constrained CAD sketches using Transformer, decomposing into primitives + constraints. Category: Constraint-Aware Generation.

5. Xie & Ju (2025) - Text-to-CadQuery: arXiv 2025. Directly generates CadQuery Python code from text, 170k text-CadQuery pairs, best model 69.3% exact match. Category: Text-to-CAD Code Generation.

6. Wang et al. (2025) - CADFusion: arXiv 2025. LLM-based text-to-CAD with alternating sequential learning and visual feedback via DPO. Category: Text-to-CAD / Visual Feedback.

7. Govindarajan et al. (2025) - CADmium: arXiv 2025. Fine-tunes code-LLMs (Qwen2.5-Coder) on 170k CAD models with GPT-4.1 annotations, JSON-based format, introduces geometric/topological eval metrics. Category: LLM Fine-tuning / Evaluation.

8. Badagabettu et al. (2024) - Query2CAD: arXiv 2024. Zero-shot LLM generates FreeCAD macros with self-refinement loops using BLIP2 feedback. 53.6% first-attempt success, +23.1% with refinement. Category: Agentic Design Loops.

9. Alam & Ahmed (2024/2025) - GenCAD: TMLR 2025. Image-conditioned CAD generation using autoregressive transformers + contrastive learning + latent diffusion. Category: Image-to-CAD / B-rep Generation.

10. Fan et al. (2025) - CADDesigner: arXiv 2025. LLM-powered agent for conceptual CAD with multimodal input, interactive dialogue, knowledge base, ECIP paradigm. Category: Agentic CAD Design.

11. Li et al. (2025) - Seek-CAD: arXiv 2025. Training-free approach using locally deployed DeepSeek-R1 with visual+CoT self-refinement. RAG over CAD code corpus. Category: Training-Free / Agentic Design.

12. Daareyni et al. (2025) - Generative AI meets CAD: Int J Adv Manuf Tech 2025. Open-source Python/Streamlit app integrating GPT with OpenSCAD, 3-layer feedback loop, design-to-manufacturing. Category: Open-Source Pipeline / DfAM.

13. Tsuji et al. (2025) - GenCAD-Self-Repairing: arXiv 2025. Self-repair pipeline for infeasible B-reps using guided diffusion + regression correction. ~10% baseline infeasibility, converts 2/3 to feasible. Category: Feasibility / B-rep Generation.

14. Heidari & Iosifidis (2025) - Geometric Deep Learning for CAD Survey: IEEE Access 2025. Comprehensive survey of GDL for CAD covering datasets (ABC 1M+ models, SketchGraphs 15M sketches, Fusion 360), methods, benchmarks. Category: Survey / Benchmark Overview.

15. Dong et al. (2025) - HistCAD: arXiv 2025. 170,236 executable constraint-aware parametric CAD sequences, Constraint-Aware Editability Benchmark with ER/cPCSR/OES metrics. Category: Dataset / Constraint-Aware Evaluation."}
function.name: create_artifact

messages.content:
### Artifact (ID artifact-00)

| Reference # | Citation | Category | Summary (3-5 sentences) | Relevance to Python/Code-Based Generative CAD Pipeline for Powder-Dosing Device |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 1 | Wu et al. (2021) (pqac-00000021, pqac-00000022, pqac-00000024) | Landmark Dataset / Foundational Model | Introduces the first deep generative network that explicitly outputs CAD construction sequences rather than discrete shape representations. Uses a Transformer-based autoencoder to encode and decode parameterized commands. Released a massive dataset of 178,238 CAD models paired with command sequences. | Provides the foundational sequence-based representation and dataset scale needed to train neural CAD engines. |
| 2 | Willis et al. (2021) (pqac-00000011) | Landmark Dataset / Benchmark | Introduces a large dataset of 8,625 human-designed CAD programs encoded as sketch-and-extrude sequences. Provides the Fusion 360 Gym execution environment that frames sequential construction as a Markov decision process. Enables benchmarking CAD reconstruction from target geometry using neurally-guided search. | Useful for understanding human design patterns and structuring reinforcement learning environments for code generation. |
| 3 | Seff et al. (2021) (pqac-00000028) | Constraint-Aware Sketch Generation / Dataset | Presents an autoregressive generative model for parametric CAD sketches that outputs both geometric primitives and explicit constraints. Trained on the SketchGraphs dataset comprising millions of real-world sketches. Uses self-attention to propagate state and outputs graphs compatible with standard constraint solvers. | Crucial for ensuring that generated 2D profiles for device components maintain valid geometric constraints before being extruded into 3D. |
| 4 | Para et al. (2021) (pqac-00000027, pqac-00000029) | Constraint-Aware Generation | A generative framework that outputs constrained sketches by decomposing the generation into primitives and their respective constraints. Uses a transformer architecture capturing long-range dependencies over a sequential token representation. Employs continuous parameter quantization followed by traditional constraint solvers to refine outputs. | Directly applicable for robustly generating and constraining 2D base profiles for mechanical parts. |
| 5 | Xie & Ju (2025) (pqac-00000000, pqac-00000005, pqac-00000006) | Text-to-CAD Code Generation | Proposes generating executable CadQuery Python code directly from natural language prompts using LLMs. Releases a massive dataset of 170k text-to-CadQuery pairs and fine-tunes models up to 7B parameters. Achieves a 69.3% exact match rate, demonstrating that scaling pretrained LLMs yields highly executable code. | Highly relevant as it directly outputs pure Python (CadQuery) scripts, perfectly aligning with a programmatic open-source CAD pipeline. |
| 6 | Wang et al. (2025) (pqac-00000019, pqac-00000032) | Text-to-CAD / Visual Feedback | An LLM-based text-to-CAD framework that integrates visual feedback to guide generation. Alternates between sequential learning on ground-truth parametric sequences and visual feedback stages using preference optimization (DPO). Rewards generated sequences that render into visually preferred models, overcoming the many-to-one rendering ambiguity. | Highlights how visual validation loops can improve the physical accuracy and visual fidelity of generated device components. |
| 7 | Govindarajan et al. (2025) (pqac-00000036) | LLM Fine-tuning / Evaluation | Reformulates CAD generation as a pure text-to-text task by fine-tuning code-LLMs (like Qwen2.5-Coder) on 170k models annotated with GPT-4.1. Uses a minimal JSON-based format to represent sequential CAD designs. Introduces advanced geometric and topological metrics (sphericity, mean curvature, Euler characteristic) to evaluate generated structures. | Demonstrates effective fine-tuning strategies for code-LLMs and provides rigorous metrics to validate generated mechanical parts. |
| 8 | Badagabettu et al. (2024) (pqac-00000038) | Agentic Design Loops | A zero-shot LLM framework that generates Python macros for FreeCAD based on natural language queries. Integrates a self-refinement loop that uses BLIP2 visual question-answering feedback to iterate on designs. Achieves a 53.6% success rate on the first attempt, improving by 23.1% after refinement iterations. | Directly matches the goal of creating a Python-based open-source CAD generator, providing a blueprint for agentic refinement loops. |
| 9 | Alam & Ahmed (2024) (pqac-00000035) | Image-to-CAD / B-rep Generation | An image-conditioned generative model that outputs parametric CAD command sequences for editable 3D shapes. Combines autoregressive transformers, a contrastive learning framework, and latent diffusion priors. Outperforms existing methods on unconditional and conditional CAD model generation tasks. | Demonstrates how multimodal inputs (like sketches or images of a powder device) can be translated into parametric code sequences. |
| 10 | Fan et al. (2025) (pqac-00000014, pqac-00000020) | Agentic CAD Design | An LLM-powered agent for conceptual CAD design that processes multimodal inputs and engages in interactive dialogue. Uses an Explicit Context Imperative Paradigm (ECIP) to decouple complex operations and stores cases in a knowledge base for improved generation. Integrates iterative visual feedback to refine the fidelity of the generated models. | Provides an architecture for an interactive, agentic design loop that can iteratively refine mechanical component code. |
| 11 | Li et al. (2025) (pqac-00000034) | Training-Free / Agentic Design | A training-free generative CAD approach using the locally deployed DeepSeek-R1 open-source model. Leverages retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) over a CAD code corpus and a step-wise visual/Chain-of-Thought feedback loop to self-refine outputs. Introduces a new dataset organized around a Sketch-SketchFeature-Refinement (SSR) paradigm. | Highly relevant for building cost-effective, locally-hosted generative pipelines using open-source reasoning models. |
| 12 | Daareyni et al. (2025) (pqac-00000016, pqac-00000033) | Open-Source Pipeline / DfAM | Presents an open-source Python/Streamlit application linking GPT-4 with OpenSCAD for text/sketch-to-CAD workflows. Implements a three-layer closed-loop feedback mechanism involving syntax checks, LLM self-evaluation, and user feedback. Evaluates LLM-assisted CAD modeling for direct design-to-manufacturing applications. | Provides a practical, open-source architectural blueprint for translating AI outputs into manufacturable scripts. |
| 13 | Tsuji et al. (2025) (pqac-00000018) | Feasibility / B-rep Generation | Addresses the limitation that generative CAD models often produce infeasible boundary representations (~10% failure rate). Proposes a framework utilizing guided diffusion denoising and regression-based self-repair to correct faulty command sequences. Converts two-thirds of previously infeasible designs into viable, geometrically accurate models. | Critical for ensuring that AI-generated scripts for physical devices compile into manufacturable and watertight solids. |
| 14 | Heidari & Iosifidis (2025) (pqac-00000010, pqac-00000012, pqac-00000037) | Survey / Benchmark Overview | A comprehensive review of geometric deep learning applied to CAD. Details major benchmark datasets such as the ABC dataset (1M+ B-rep models), SketchGraphs (15M constraint graphs), and Fusion 360 Gallery. Highlights the shift from manual heuristics to automated feature representation and constraint synthesis. | Acts as a central reference point for understanding the landscape of datasets and benchmarks available for training CAD generators. |
| 15 | Dong et al. (2025) (pqac-00000026, pqac-00000031) | Dataset / Constraint-Aware Evaluation | Introduces a massive dataset of 170,236 executable constraint-aware parametric CAD sequences to bridge the gap between pure geometry and editability. Flattens sketch geometry into explicit geometric and dimensional constraint graphs. Presents a Constraint-Aware Editability Benchmark to evaluate how well models preserve design intent during edits. | Essential for ensuring that generated programmatic code includes valid constraints, keeping the open-source device editable and robust. |


*Table: A structured summary of key academic papers from 2021-2025 focusing on generative CAD, constraint-aware sketching, and LLM-driven code generation, mapped to their relevance for programmatic hardware design.*

Artifact IDs that may be injected into the answer: [artifact-00]

function.arguments:
{"answer": "## Literature Review: Generative CAD, AI-Assisted CAD, and LLM-Based Code Generation for Engineering Design (2021–2025)

### 1. Introduction

The rapid convergence of large language models (LLMs), deep generative architectures, and parametric computer-aided design (CAD) has created an emerging field of generative CAD that promises to transform engineering design workflows. This review surveys 15 key publications from 2021–2025 covering foundational datasets/benchmarks, text-to-CAD generation, constraint-aware sketch synthesis, agentic design loops with visual feedback, and the principal limitations of current approaches. Each entry is assessed for its relevance to building a Python/code-based generative CAD pipeline for an open-source powder-dosing device.

The following table provides a structured overview of all 15 references, organized by category, with summaries and relevance assessments.

<artifact id="artifact-00"/>

### 2. Foundational Datasets and Benchmarks

**DeepCAD** (Wu et al., ICCV 2021) established the first large-scale dataset of 178,238 CAD models represented as parameterized command sequences (sketch + extrude operations). The Transformer-based autoencoder treats CAD operations analogously to natural language tokens, with continuous parameters quantized into 256 levels to convert regression into classification (pqac-00000021, pqac-00000022, pqac-00000024). Evaluation metrics introduced include Chamfer Distance (CD), Command Accuracy (~99.3%), Parameter Accuracy (~97.3%), and Invalid Ratio (2.7–3.5%) (pqac-00000023). DeepCAD remains the most widely used training corpus for generative CAD research.

**Fusion 360 Gallery** (Willis et al., ACM TOG 2021) provides 8,625 human-designed CAD programs in a compact sketch-and-extrude language, alongside the Fusion 360 Gym interactive environment that exposes construction as a Markov decision process (pqac-00000011). The dataset also includes an assembly subset with 8,251 assemblies and 154,468 parts with joint annotations (pqac-00000009, pqac-00000012). It remains the primary benchmark for CAD reconstruction from target geometry.

**SketchGraphs** consists of 15 million real-world parametric 2D sketches from Onshape, each represented as a geometric constraint graph with nodes (primitives) and edges (constraints such as coincidence, tangency, and parallelism) (pqac-00000010, pqac-00000030). An open-source Python processing pipeline is provided. The **ABC Dataset** contributes over 1 million high-quality B-rep CAD models with precisely parameterized curves and surfaces, though it lacks construction histories (pqac-00000008, pqac-00000009, pqac-00000010).

### 3. Text-to-CAD and LLM-Based Code Generation

The most directly relevant paradigm for a Python-based pipeline is **Text-to-CadQuery** (Xie & Ju, 2025), which generates executable CadQuery Python code directly from natural language descriptions. The authors augmented the DeepCAD/Text2CAD data with 170,000 CadQuery annotations and fine-tuned six LLMs (124M–7B parameters), achieving a top-1 exact match of 69.3% and a 48.6% reduction in Chamfer Distance (pqac-00000005, pqac-00000006). A fine-tuned GPT-4.1 mini achieved a 0% invalid rate, demonstrating that larger models can produce fully executable code (pqac-00000005). This approach leverages the fact that foundation LLMs already excel at Python generation and spatial reasoning (pqac-00000006).

**CADmium** (Govindarajan et al., 2025) reformulates CAD generation as a text-to-text task by fine-tuning code-LLMs such as Qwen2.5-Coder using LoRA on 170k+ models annotated with GPT-4.1-generated descriptions. It uses a JSON-based format for CAD sequences and introduces geometric and topological evaluation metrics based on sphericity, mean curvature, and Euler characteristic (pqac-00000036).

**Query2CAD** (Badagabettu et al., 2024) demonstrates a zero-shot approach where GPT-4 Turbo generates Python macros for FreeCAD without supervised training. It implements self-refinement loops using BLIP2 visual question-answering feedback, achieving 53.6% success on the first attempt and improving by 23.1% through iterative refinement (pqac-00000038).

**Daareyni et al. (2025)** present GAD, an open-source Python/Streamlit application integrating GPT-4 with OpenSCAD for multimodal text/sketch/voice-to-CAD workflows. The system implements a three-layer closed-loop feedback mechanism (syntax checking, LLM self-evaluation, and user feedback) and supports direct export to STL and G-code for manufacturing (pqac-00000016, pqac-00000033).

### 4. Visual Feedback and Agentic Design Loops

**CADFusion** (Wang et al., 2025) uses LLMs as the backbone and alternates between sequential learning on ground-truth parametric sequences and a visual feedback stage that rewards sequences rendering into visually preferred objects using DPO (direct preference optimization). This addresses the many-to-one rendering ambiguity inherent in CAD, where multiple parametric sequences can produce the same visual object (pqac-00000019, pqac-00000032).

**CADDesigner** (Fan et al., 2025) is an LLM-powered agent for conceptual CAD that accepts multimodal input (text + sketches), engages in interactive dialogue for requirement analysis, and supports a broad range of operations including extrusion, revolution, fillet/chamfer, sweeping, and lofting. It uses an Explicit Context Imperative Paradigm (ECIP) to decouple CAD operations and stores cases in a knowledge base for improved code generation (pqac-00000020, pqac-00000014).

**Seek-CAD** (Li et al., 2025) is a training-free approach using the locally deployed open-source DeepSeek-R1 model with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) over a CAD code corpus. It employs step-wise visual feedback combined with Chain-of-Thought reasoning from a Vision-Language Model to iteratively self-refine generated parametric CAD code (pqac-00000034).

### 5. Constraint-Aware Sketch and Shape Generation

**SketchGen** (Para et al., 2021) addresses constrained sketch generation by decomposing the task into primitive generation and conditional constraint generation, p(S) = p(C|P)p(P), using two autoregressive Transformer networks. Continuous parameters are aggressively quantized during generation, then refined by a traditional constraint solver to correct discretization errors (pqac-00000027, pqac-00000025, pqac-00000029).

**Vitruvion** (Seff et al., 2021) similarly autoregressively generates both primitives and explicit constraints trained on SketchGraphs, but additionally supports conditioning on hand-drawn sketches for reverse engineering from noisy inputs. Its outputs are constraint-graph representations compatible with standard CAD constraint solvers, enabling import, solving, and subsequent editable modifications (pqac-00000028).

**HistCAD** (Dong et al., 2025) introduces 170,236 executable constraint-aware parametric CAD sequences in a CAD-backend-independent intermediate language. It explicitly encodes 19 constraint types and defines a Constraint-Aware Editability Benchmark with metrics for Edit Reachability (ER), Conditional Preserved Constraint Satisfaction Rate (cPCSR), and Overall Editable Success (OES), demonstrating that explicit constraints are essential for preserving design intent after parameter edits (pqac-00000026, pqac-00000031).

### 6. Image-to-CAD and B-rep Generation

**GenCAD** (Alam & Ahmed, 2024/2025) combines autoregressive transformers, contrastive learning, and latent diffusion priors to generate parametric CAD command sequences conditioned on images. It significantly outperforms prior methods on both unconditional and conditional generation tasks while also enabling image-based retrieval from large CAD databases (pqac-00000035).

**GenCAD-Self-Repairing** (Tsuji et al., 2025) addresses the critical limitation that approximately 10% of generated B-rep designs are infeasible. It proposes a guided diffusion denoising process in latent space combined with regression-based correction to repair infeasible command sequences, converting two-thirds of infeasible designs to feasible ones while maintaining geometric accuracy (pqac-00000018).

### 7. Survey Coverage

**Heidari & Iosifidis (2025)** provide a comprehensive survey of geometric deep learning for CAD, covering similarity analysis, retrieval, 2D/3D model synthesis, and generation from point clouds and images. The survey catalogs all major benchmark datasets—ABC (1M+ models), SketchGraphs (15M sketches), Fusion 360 Gallery—and notes that public CAD databases are predominantly unlabeled, posing obstacles for supervised learning (pqac-00000010, pqac-00000012, pqac-00000037).

### 8. Key Capabilities

Current systems demonstrate several capabilities relevant to building a generative CAD pipeline:

- **Text-to-CAD code generation**: Direct generation of executable Python (CadQuery, OpenSCAD, FreeCAD macros) from natural language, with best models achieving 69.3% exact match and 0% invalid rate at scale (pqac-00000005, pqac-00000006).
- **Constraint-aware generation**: Explicit constraint graphs for sketch primitives enabling valid geometric relationships (parallelism, tangency, concentricity) that are essential for manufacturable parts (pqac-00000027, pqac-00000028, pqac-00000026).
- **Agentic design loops**: Self-refinement via visual feedback (VLM rendering + VQA scoring), Chain-of-Thought reasoning, and human-in-the-loop mechanisms that iteratively improve generated designs (pqac-00000013, pqac-00000032, pqac-00000034, pqac-00000038).
- **Design-to-manufacturing pipelines**: Open-source frameworks supporting direct export to STL, G-code, and connections to 3D printers (pqac-00000033).

### 9. Key Limitations

Several persistent limitations must be addressed for practical deployment:

- **Geometric hallucination**: LLMs may incorrectly generate circular profiles for hexagonal nuts, miss flange boreholes, or produce incorrect spring helices due to limited geometric reasoning (pqac-00000013, pqac-00000002).
- **Manufacturability gaps**: Generated models often lack validation for wall thickness, overhang angles, support structures, and other additive manufacturing constraints. Virtually approved designs can fail physically (pqac-00000015, pqac-00000014).
- **Parametric editability**: Training solely on parametric sequences may bias models toward training-set patterns, overlooking valid alternative constructions. Without explicit constraints, edits cause geometric distortion and loss of perpendicularity or tangency (pqac-00000019, pqac-00000026).
- **B-rep feasibility**: Approximately 10% of generated B-reps are infeasible, and the likelihood of invalid models increases with longer, more complex command sequences (pqac-00000018, pqac-00000013).
- **Evaluation challenges**: Standard metrics (Chamfer Distance, IoU) often fail to capture structural or topological quality. Newer metrics based on sphericity, mean curvature, and Euler characteristic are being introduced (pqac-00000036, pqac-00000023).
- **Dataset limitations**: Existing datasets are biased toward simple shapes, exclude certain CAD features (fillets, chamfers), and lack natural language annotations or manufacturing metadata (pqac-00000002, pqac-00000001).

### 10. Implications for a Python/Code-Based Generative CAD Pipeline for an Open-Source Powder-Dosing Device

For building a generative CAD pipeline targeting a powder-dosing device, several architectural recommendations emerge from this literature:

1. **Output representation**: CadQuery Python code is the most promising target, as it is a pure-Python, open-source API that does not require proprietary CAD software, and pretrained LLMs already demonstrate strong Python generation capabilities (pqac-00000006).
2. **Self-refinement**: Implementing an agentic loop with visual feedback (rendering the generated model and using a VLM to verify geometric fidelity) is critical, as first-attempt success rates remain modest (53–69%) but improve substantially with iteration (pqac-00000038, pqac-00000013).
3. **Constraint encoding**: Explicit geometric constraints should be embedded in the generation pipeline to preserve design intent and enable parametric editing of critical dimensions such as chamber volume, orifice diameter, and wall thickness (pqac-00000026, pqac-00000028).
4. **Feasibility checking**: A post-generation validation step—either solver-based or diffusion-guided self-repair—is essential to ensure watertight, manufacturable B-reps suitable for 3D printing (pqac-00000018).
5. **Open-source toolchain**: The GAD architecture (Python/Streamlit + OpenSCAD + GPT API) provides a practical starting template, while Seek-CAD demonstrates that locally deployed open-source reasoning models can achieve competitive performance without API costs (pqac-00000033, pqac-00000034).
6. **Training data**: The DeepCAD corpus (178k models) and Text-to-CadQuery annotations (170k pairs) provide the most immediately usable training resources, though domain-specific fine-tuning on mechanical/dosing-device geometries would likely be necessary (pqac-00000021, pqac-00000000)."}

It implements self-refinement loops using BLIP2 visual question-answering feedback, achieving 53.6% success on the first attempt and improving by 23.1% through iterative refinement (pqac-00000038).

**Daareyni et al. (2025)** present GAD, an open-source Python/Streamlit application integrating GPT-4 with OpenSCAD for multimodal text/sketch/voice-to-CAD workflows. The system implements a three-layer closed-loop feedback mechanism (syntax checking, LLM self-evaluation, and user feedback) and supports direct export to STL and G-code for manufacturing (pqac-00000016, pqac-00000033).

### 4. Visual Feedback and Agentic Design Loops

**CADFusion** (Wang et al., 2025) uses LLMs as the backbone and alternates between sequential learning on ground-truth parametric sequences and a visual feedback stage that rewards sequences rendering into visually preferred objects using DPO (direct preference optimization). This addresses the many-to-one rendering ambiguity inherent in CAD, where multiple parametric sequences can produce the same visual object (pqac-00000019, pqac-00000032).

**CADDesigner** (Fan et al., 2025) is an LLM-powered agent for conceptual CAD that accepts multimodal input (text + sketches), engages in interactive dialogue for requirement analysis, and supports a broad range of operations including extrusion, revolution, fillet/chamfer, sweeping, and lofting. It uses an Explicit Context Imperative Paradigm (ECIP) to decouple CAD operations and stores cases in a knowledge base for improved code generation (pqac-00000020, pqac-00000014).

**Seek-CAD** (Li et al., 2025) is a training-free approach using the locally deployed open-source DeepSeek-R1 model with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) over a CAD code corpus. It employs step-wise visual feedback combined with Chain-of-Thought reasoning from a Vision-Language Model to iteratively self-refine generated parametric CAD code (pqac-00000034).

### 5. Constraint-Aware Sketch and Shape Generation

**SketchGen** (Para et al., 2021) addresses constrained sketch generation by decomposing the task into primitive generation and conditional constraint generation, p(S) = p(C|P)p(P), using two autoregressive Transformer networks. Continuous parameters are aggressively quantized during generation, then refined by a traditional constraint solver to correct discretization errors (pqac-00000027, pqac-00000025, pqac-00000029).

**Vitruvion** (Seff et al., 2021) similarly autoregressively generates both primitives and explicit constraints trained on SketchGraphs, but additionally supports conditioning on hand-drawn sketches for reverse engineering from noisy inputs. Its outputs are constraint-graph representations compatible with standard CAD constraint solvers, enabling import, solving, and subsequent editable modifications (pqac-00000028).

**HistCAD** (Dong et al., 2025) introduces 170,236 executable constraint-aware parametric CAD sequences in a CAD-backend-independent intermediate language. It explicitly encodes 19 constraint types and defines a Constraint-Aware Editability Benchmark with metrics for Edit Reachability (ER), Conditional Preserved Constraint Satisfaction Rate (cPCSR), and Overall Editable Success (OES), demonstrating that explicit constraints are essential for preserving design intent after parameter edits (pqac-00000026, pqac-00000031).

### 6. Image-to-CAD and B-rep Generation

**GenCAD** (Alam & Ahmed, 2024/2025) combines autoregressive transformers, contrastive learning, and latent diffusion priors to generate parametric CAD command sequences conditioned on images. It significantly outperforms prior methods on both unconditional and conditional generation tasks while also enabling image-based retrieval from large CAD databases (pqac-00000035).

**GenCAD-Self-Repairing** (Tsuji et al., 2025) addresses the critical limitation that approximately 10% of generated B-rep designs are infeasible. It proposes a guided diffusion denoising process in latent space combined with regression-based correction to repair infeasible command sequences, converting two-thirds of infeasible designs to feasible ones while maintaining geometric accuracy (pqac-00000018).

### 7. Survey Coverage

**Heidari & Iosifidis (2025)** provide a comprehensive survey of geometric deep learning for CAD, covering similarity analysis, retrieval, 2D/3D model synthesis, and generation from point clouds and images. The survey catalogs all major benchmark datasets—ABC (1M+ models), SketchGraphs (15M sketches), Fusion 360 Gallery—and notes that public CAD databases are predominantly unlabeled, posing obstacles for supervised learning (pqac-00000010, pqac-00000012, pqac-00000037).

### 8. Key Capabilities

Current systems demonstrate several capabilities relevant to building a generative CAD pipeline:

- **Text-to-CAD code generation**: Direct generation of executable Python (CadQuery, OpenSCAD, FreeCAD macros) from natural language, with best models achieving 69.3% exact match and 0% invalid rate at scale (pqac-00000005, pqac-00000006).
- **Constraint-aware generation**: Explicit constraint graphs for sketch primitives enabling valid geometric relationships (parallelism, tangency, concentricity) that are essential for manufacturable parts (pqac-00000027, pqac-00000028, pqac-00000026).
- **Agentic design loops**: Self-refinement via visual feedback (VLM rendering + VQA scoring), Chain-of-Thought reasoning, and human-in-the-loop mechanisms that iteratively improve generated designs (pqac-00000013, pqac-00000032, pqac-00000034, pqac-00000038).
- **Design-to-manufacturing pipelines**: Open-source frameworks supporting direct export to STL, G-code, and connections to 3D printers (pqac-00000033).

### 9. Key Limitations

Several persistent limitations must be addressed for practical deployment:

- **Geometric hallucination**: LLMs may incorrectly generate circular profiles for hexagonal nuts, miss flange boreholes, or produce incorrect spring helices due to limited geometric reasoning (pqac-00000013, pqac-00000002).
- **Manufacturability gaps**: Generated models often lack validation for wall thickness, overhang angles, support structures, and other additive manufacturing constraints. Virtually approved designs can fail physically (pqac-00000015, pqac-00000014).
- **Parametric editability**: Training solely on parametric sequences may bias models toward training-set patterns, overlooking valid alternative constructions. Without explicit constraints, edits cause geometric distortion and loss of perpendicularity or tangency (pqac-00000019, pqac-00000026).
- **B-rep feasibility**: Approximately 10% of generated B-reps are infeasible, and the likelihood of invalid models increases with longer, more complex command sequences (pqac-00000018, pqac-00000013).
- **Evaluation challenges**: Standard metrics (Chamfer Distance, IoU) often fail to capture structural or topological quality. Newer metrics based on sphericity, mean curvature, and Euler characteristic are being introduced (pqac-00000036, pqac-00000023).
- **Dataset limitations**: Existing datasets are biased toward simple shapes, exclude certain CAD features (fillets, chamfers), and lack natural language annotations or manufacturing metadata (pqac-00000002, pqac-00000001).

### 10. Implications for a Python/Code-Based Generative CAD Pipeline for an Open-Source Powder-Dosing Device

For building a generative CAD pipeline targeting a powder-dosing device, several architectural recommendations emerge from this literature:

1. **Output representation**: CadQuery Python code is the most promising target, as it is a pure-Python, open-source API that does not require proprietary CAD software, and pretrained LLMs already demonstrate strong Python generation capabilities (pqac-00000006).
2. **Self-refinement**: Implementing an agentic loop with visual feedback (rendering the generated model and using a VLM to verify geometric fidelity) is critical, as first-attempt success rates remain modest (53–69%) but improve substantially with iteration (pqac-00000038, pqac-00000013).
3. **Constraint encoding**: Explicit geometric constraints should be embedded in the generation pipeline to preserve design intent and enable parametric editing of critical dimensions such as chamber volume, orifice diameter, and wall thickness (pqac-00000026, pqac-00000028).
4. **Feasibility checking**: A post-generation validation step—either solver-based or diffusion-guided self-repair—is essential to ensure watertight, manufacturable B-reps suitable for 3D printing (pqac-00000018).
5. **Open-source toolchain**: The GAD architecture (Python/Streamlit + OpenSCAD + GPT API) provides a practical starting template, while Seek-CAD demonstrates that locally deployed open-source reasoning models can achieve competitive performance without API costs (pqac-00000033, pqac-00000034).
6. **Training data**: The DeepCAD corpus (178k models) and Text-to-CadQuery annotations (170k pairs) provide the most immediately usable training resources, though domain-specific fine-tuning on mechanical/dosing-device geometries would likely be necessary (pqac-00000021, pqac-00000000)."}

## Literature Review: Generative CAD, AI-Assisted CAD, and LLM-Based Code Generation for Engineering Design (2021–2025)

### 1. Introduction

The rapid convergence of large language models (LLMs), deep generative architectures, and parametric computer-aided design (CAD) has created an emerging field of generative CAD that promises to transform engineering design workflows. This review surveys 15 key publications from 2021–2025 covering foundational datasets/benchmarks, text-to-CAD generation, constraint-aware sketch synthesis, agentic design loops with visual feedback, and the principal limitations of current approaches. Each entry is assessed for its relevance to building a Python/code-based generative CAD pipeline for an open-source powder-dosing device.

The following table provides a structured overview of all 15 references, organized by category, with summaries and relevance assessments.

Uses a Transformer-based autoencoder to encode and decode parameterized commands. Released a massive dataset of 178,238 CAD models paired with command sequences. | Provides the foundational sequence-based representation and dataset scale needed to train neural CAD engines. |

Trained on the SketchGraphs dataset comprising millions of real-world sketches. Uses self-attention to propagate state and outputs graphs compatible with standard constraint solvers. | Crucial for ensuring that generated 2D profiles for device components maintain valid geometric constraints before being extruded into 3D. |

(2025) (wang2501texttocadgenerationthrough pages 2-3, wang2501texttocadgenerationthrough pages 3-4) | Text-to-CAD / Visual Feedback | An LLM-based text-to-CAD framework that integrates visual feedback to guide generation. Uses a minimal JSON-based format to represent sequential CAD designs. (2024) (badagabettu2024query2cadgeneratingcad pages 1-3) | Agentic Design Loops | A zero-shot LLM framework that generates Python macros for FreeCAD based on natural language queries. Integrates a self-refinement loop that uses BLIP2 visual question-answering feedback to iterate on designs. Achieves a 53.6% success rate on the first attempt, improving by 23.1% after refinement iterations. | Directly matches the goal of creating a Python-based open-source CAD generator, providing a blueprint for agentic refinement loops. |
| 9 | Alam & Ahmed (2024) (alam2409gencadimageconditionedcomputeraided pages 1-3) | Image-to-CAD / B-rep Generation | An image-conditioned generative model that outputs parametric CAD command sequences for editable 3D shapes. Evaluates LLM-assisted CAD modeling for direct design-to-manufacturing applications. | Provides a practical, open-source architectural blueprint for translating AI outputs into manufacturable scripts. |
| 13 | Tsuji et al. (2025) (tsuji2025gencadselfrepairingfeasibilityenhancement pages 1-2) | Feasibility / B-rep Generation | Addresses the limitation that generative CAD models often produce infeasible boundary representations (~10% failure rate).
